indicator.name: Curiosity and Interest
indicator.description: Express interest in some familiar and new objects, people, and activities in their immediate environment. Seek information by exploring with their senses, describing their observations, and asking simple questions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Curiosity and Interest Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Initiative
indicator.description: Demonstrate initiative by starting activities (such as simple play scenarios), initiating social interactions (such as helping others), and seeking solutions to problems.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Initiative Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Engagement
indicator.description: Actively engage by focusing and concentrating on activities for brief periods of time with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Engagement Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Persisting Despite Difficulties
indicator.description: Demonstrate persistence, with adult support, when engaging in an activity despite encountering setbacks or boredom. Make an effort, with adult support, to cope with emotions that arise (for instance, frustration, sadness, anger, excitement), although may shift to another activity after a short while.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Persisting Despite Difficulties Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Working Memory
indicator.description: Hold approximately one to two pieces of information in their mind for a short time and use the knowledge to guide behavior when performing tasks and engaging in play, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Working Memory Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Managing Impulsive Behaviors
indicator.description: Demonstrate emerging ability to manage habitual reactions (habits that are not useful in a particular context), impulsive behaviors (acting on a whim), and delaying gratification (resisting temptation for an instant reward) with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Managing Impulsive Behaviors Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Managing Attention and Distractions
indicator.description: Demonstrate an emerging ability to ignore distractions and interruptions during independent or group activities; however, often need adult support to manage attention when distracted or interrupted.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Managing Attention and Distractions Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Flexibility
indicator.description: Demonstrate emerging ability to shift behavior and think about things in a new way, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Flexibility Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Planning
indicator.description: Demonstrate emerging ability to set and carry out simple goals (for example, one- to two-step plans and goals), with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Planning ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate emerging ability to set and carry out simple goals

indicator.name: Planning
indicator.description: Demonstrate ability to set and carry out more complex plans (for example, two- to three-step plans and goals), with less adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Planning ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate ability to set and carry out more complex plans

indicator.name: Reflecting and Analyzing
indicator.description: Make attempts to adjust a problem-solving approach or strategy by reflecting on and analyzing their current approach, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Reflecting and Analyzing ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Reflecting and Analyzing
indicator.description: Make attempts to adjust a problem-solving approach or strategy by reflecting on and analyzing their current approach, with less adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Reflecting and Analyzing ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Problem-Solving Together
indicator.description: Collaborate with peers in problem-solving during play and learning activities, sometimes for a short period of time and sometimes longer.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Problem-Solving Together ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Problem-Solving Together
indicator.description: Engage in extended collaborative problem-solving during play and learning activities. Communicate with peers about how to solve a problem and help peers when needed.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Problem-Solving Together ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Others
indicator.description: Notice, with adult support, that others have different approaches and preferences, although often have difficulty accepting others' approaches or preferences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Understanding Others ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Others
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding and explain that others can have different approaches and preferences and more consistently accept others' approaches and preferences, with less adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Understanding Others ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Self-Identity
indicator.description: Notice and communicate the characteristics of their physical appearance related to specific identities (for example, gender, race, ethnicity). Demonstrate emerging preferences for specific activities (for example, what to play, how to dress).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Self-Identity ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Self-Identity
indicator.description: Express their personal identity (for example, gender, race, or ethnicity), including a sense of pride in their identity, and communicate preferences of their appearance or activities they enjoy (for example, sharing their family's practices or their own preferences).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Self-Identity ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Confidence in Abilities
indicator.description: Describe their abilities positively and enjoy demonstrating them. Communicate the desire to be viewed positively by familiar adults, including teachers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Confidence in Abilities ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Confidence in Abilities
indicator.description: Express confidence in their abilities and describe their strengths, including reference to past abilities. Continue to be sensitive to how they are viewed by peers and familiar adults, including teachers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Confidence in Abilities ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Emotions in Self and Others
indicator.description: Identify basic emotions (for example, happy, sad, surprised) and recognize emotional expressions in self and others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Understanding Emotions in Self and Others ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Emotions in Self and Others
indicator.description: Identify basic and complex emotions (for example, embarrassment, prideful) and recognize emotional expressions in self and others. Demonstrate increasing understanding of different ways of expressing emotions and related behaviors for themselves and others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Understanding Emotions in Self and Others ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Regulating Emotions, Behaviors, and Stress
indicator.description: Make efforts to regulate their emotions and behaviors and cope with stress with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Regulating Emotions, Behaviors, and Stress ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Regulating Emotions, Behaviors, and Stress
indicator.description: Regulate emotions and behaviors and demonstrate strategies to cope with emotions and behaviors caused by stress with some adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Regulating Emotions, Behaviors, and Stress ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Managing Routines and Transitions
indicator.description: Manage routines and transitions between settings (for example, from home to child care) or activities (for example, from playtime to story time) with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Managing Routines and Transitions ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Managing Routines and Transitions
indicator.description: Anticipate routines and manage transitions between settings (for example, from home to school) or activities (for example, from snack time to small-group activities) with some adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Managing Routines and Transitions ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Awareness of Similarities and Differences Across People
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness and interest in how people are similar and different.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Awareness of Similarities and Differences Across People ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Awareness of Similarities and Differences Across People
indicator.description: Make comparisons between themselves and others and express beliefs about themselves and others based on perceived similarities and differences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Awareness of Similarities and Differences Across People ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Other People's Thoughts, Behaviors, and Experiences
indicator.description: Develop understanding that others have unique thoughts, behaviors, and experiences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Understanding Other People's Thoughts, Behaviors, and Experiences ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Understanding Other People's Thoughts, Behaviors, and Experiences
indicator.description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of the mental and psychological reasons people act as they do and how these reasons contribute to differences in how people act or behave.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Understanding Other People's Thoughts, Behaviors, and Experiences ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Empathy and Caring
indicator.description: Respond to others' distress and needs with sympathetic caring and assistance by comforting and helping others, although occasionally require support from an adult to assist.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Empathy and Caring ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Empathy and Caring
indicator.description: Demonstrate empathy by sharing the emotional experiences of others and showing concern for the needs of others in distress.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Empathy and Caring ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Reciprocal Interactions with Adults
indicator.description: Engage in positive interactions with familiar adults, especially in familiar settings. Demonstrate an emerging ability to initiate social interaction with familiar adults.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Reciprocal Interactions with Adults ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Reciprocal Interactions with Adults
indicator.description: Take greater initiative and participate in more reciprocal interactions with familiar adults (for example, initiate a conversation, suggest a shared activity, or ask for assistance).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Reciprocal Interactions with Adults ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)
Foundation 1.1 Self-Identity

indicator.name: Understanding and Using Size and Location Words
indicator.description: Understand and use words to describe the size and location of objects (such as "tiny" and "on"), including simple comparisons (such as "bigger"). Understand and use increasingly specific words to describe and compare the size and location of objects (such as "longer" and "between").

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Understanding and Using Size and Location Words

indicator.name: Using Grammatical Features and Sentence Structure
indicator.description: Use common word forms and sentence forms to express thoughts and ideas. Use both common and less common word forms and sentence forms to express complex thoughts and ideas. Refer to the full draft of the PTKLF for additional information on how this foundation develops depending on the languages children are learning.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Using Grammatical Features and Sentence Structure*

indicator.name: Asking Questions
indicator.description: Use questions to seek information and to clarify and confirm understanding. Use questions and follow-up questions to seek information and to clarify and confirm understanding.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Asking Questions

indicator.name: Constructing Narratives
indicator.description: Use language to construct real or fictional short narratives. Use language to construct real or fictional extended narratives that have several details or a plotline.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Constructing Narratives

indicator.name: Sharing Explanations and Opinions
indicator.description: Share descriptions, opinions, and explanations. Share detailed descriptions, opinions, and explanations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Sharing Explanations and Opinions

indicator.name: Participating in Conversations
indicator.description: Participate in back-and-forth conversations with adults and peers. Respond on topic for at least one turn in a conversation. Participate in increasingly long and complex back-and-forth conversations with adults and peers. Respond on topic across several turns in the conversation.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Participating in Conversations

indicator.name: Isolating Initial Sounds
indicator.description: Match words that have the same first sound in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects. Isolate and pronounce the first sound of a word, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Isolating Initial Sounds

indicator.name: Isolating Initial Sounds
indicator.description: Match words that have the same first sound in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Isolating Initial Sounds ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Match words that have the same first sound in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicator.name: Isolating Initial Sounds
indicator.description: Isolate and pronounce the first sound of a word, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Isolating Initial Sounds ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Isolate and pronounce the first sound of a word, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Blending Sounds
indicator.description: When presented with two single-syllable words (such as "sand" and "box"), blend them into a compound word in speech with adult support or the support of pictures or objects. When presented with syllables and individual sounds, blend them into words in speech with adult support or the support of pictures or objects. Refer to the full draft of the PTKLF for additional information on how this foundation develops depending on the languages children are learning.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Recognizing and Blending Sounds*

indicator.name: Recognizing and Blending Sounds
indicator.description: When presented with two single-syllable words (such as "sand" and "box"), blend them into a compound word in speech with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Recognizing and Blending Sounds*

indicator.name: Recognizing and Blending Sounds
indicator.description: When presented with syllables and individual sounds, blend them into words in speech with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Recognizing and Blending Sounds*

indicator.name: Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay
indicator.description: Recognize or participate in familiar rhymes or songs. Produce rhyming sounds or words. Rhymes may be imperfect and can be real or nonsense words.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay

indicator.name: Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay
indicator.description: Recognize or participate in familiar rhymes or songs.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay

indicator.name: Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay
indicator.description: Produce rhyming sounds or words. Rhymes may be imperfect and can be real or nonsense words.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay

indicator.name: Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Match some letter names to their printed form. These will commonly be letters in the child's first name. If learning the alphabet in English, Spanish, or other languages using a similar alphabet, such as Tagalog, match some (about three to eight) uppercase letter names and approximately half (about 12 to 16) of the lowercase letter names to their printed form. Match many letter names to their printed form. If learning the alphabet in English, Spanish, or other languages using a similar alphabet, such as Tagalog, match most (about 15 to 20) uppercase letter names and approximately half (about 12 to 16) of the lowercase letter names to their printed form.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Identifying Letters*

indicator.name: Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Match some letter names to their printed form. These will commonly be letters in the child's first name. If learning the alphabet in English, Spanish, or other languages using a similar alphabet, such as Tagalog, match some (about three to eight) uppercase letter names to their printed form.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Identifying Letters*

indicator.name: Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Match many letter names to their printed form. If learning the alphabet in English, Spanish, or other languages using a similar alphabet, such as Tagalog, match most (about 15 to 20) uppercase letter names and approximately half (about 12 to 16) of the lowercase letter names to their printed form.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Identifying Letters*

indicator.name: Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence
indicator.description: Recognize that letters or characters have sounds.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence

indicator.name: Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence
indicator.description: Accurately identify or produce sounds associated with several letters or common characters with adult support. If learning the alphabet in English, Spanish, or other languages using a similar alphabet, such as Tagalog, accurately identify or produce sounds associated with about half of the letters.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence

indicator.name: Understanding the Concept of Print
indicator.description: Identify the meaning of a few instances of familiar print in the environment.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Understanding the Concept of Print

indicator.name: Understanding the Concept of Print
indicator.description: Recognize print as something that can be read and has meaning.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Understanding the Concept of Print

indicator.name: Understanding Print Conventions
indicator.description: Display basic book-handling behaviors and knowledge of basic print conventions, such as turning pages in a single direction and recognizing the cover and title of a book.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Understanding Print Conventions

indicator.name: Understanding Print Conventions
indicator.description: Display increasingly sophisticated book-handling behaviors and knowledge of print conventions, such as turning pages one at a time and understanding the direction and orientation of print.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Understanding Print Conventions

indicator.name: Demonstrating Interest in Literacy Activities
indicator.description: Demonstrate interest in and engagement with literacy and literacy-related activities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Demonstrating Interest in Literacy Activities

indicator.name: Demonstrating Interest in Literacy Activities
indicator.description: Demonstrate interest in and engagement with literacy and literacy-related activities for progressively extended periods of time and with increasing independence.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Demonstrating Interest in Literacy Activities

sub_strand.name: Comprehension and Analysis of Age-Appropriate Text

indicator.name: Understanding Stories
indicator.description: Demonstrate basic understanding of main characters or events in a story after the child has experienced the story a few times.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Stories

sub_strand.name: Comprehension and Analysis of Age-Appropriate Text

indicator.name: Understanding Stories
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding of details in a story, including knowledge of characters, events, and ordering of events, and use their increased understanding of story structure to predict what might come next when asked.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Stories

sub_strand.name: Comprehension and Analysis of Age-Appropriate Text

indicator.name: Understanding Informational Text
indicator.description: Demonstrate basic understanding of informational text after the child has experienced the text a few times.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Informational Text

sub_strand.name: Comprehension and Analysis of Age-Appropriate Text

indicator.name: Understanding Informational Text
indicator.description: Demonstrate deeper understanding of informational text using their abilities to make connections to previous knowledge, make inferences, and ask questions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Informational Text

indicator.name: Developing Fine Motor Skills in Writing
indicator.description: Experiment with grasp and body position using a variety of drawing and writing tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Developing Fine Motor Skills in Writing

indicator.name: Developing Fine Motor Skills in Writing
indicator.description: Adjust grasp and body position for increased control in drawing and writing.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Developing Fine Motor Skills in Writing

indicator.name: Writing to Represent Sounds
indicator.description: Write, with adult support, a few recognizable letters that are intended to represent their corresponding sounds.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Writing to Represent Sounds*

indicator.name: Dictating Thoughts and Ideas to Be Conveyed in Writing
indicator.description: Engage in dictating thoughts and ideas when an adult offers to help with writing them down.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Dictating Thoughts and Ideas to Be Conveyed in Writing

indicator.name: Dictating Thoughts and Ideas to Be Conveyed in Writing
indicator.description: Demonstrate interest in conveying extended thoughts and ideas in writing, engaging the help of an adult.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Dictating Thoughts and Ideas to Be Conveyed in Writing

indicator.name: Writing to Represent Words or Ideas
indicator.description: Write using scribbles that resemble letters or characters and are distinct from pictures.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Writing to Represent Words or Ideas*

indicator.name: Writing to Represent Words or Ideas
indicator.description: Write a few recognizable letters or characters to represent words or ideas.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Writing to Represent Words or Ideas*

indicator.name: Writing Own Name
indicator.description: Write marks to represent own name.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Writing Own Name

indicator.name: Writing Own Name
indicator.description: Write own name nearly correctly.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Writing Own Name

domain.name: Foundational Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding and Using Vocabulary
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Understand and use words for objects, actions, and attributes frequently experienced in everyday life, such as through play, conversations, or stories. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Understand and use an increasing variety of words for objects, actions, and attributes experienced in everyday life, such as through play, conversations, or stories.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Understanding and Using Vocabulary

domain.name: Foundational Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding and Using Words for Categories
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Understand and use increasingly specific

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Understanding and Using Words for Categories

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Words
indicator.description: Pay attention to English oral language and understand a few common English words, while relying mainly on intonation, facial expressions, and gestures of the speaker in interactions with adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Understanding Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Words
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding of words in English for objects and actions as well as phrases encountered frequently in interactions with adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Understanding Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Words
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding of a larger set of words in English (for example, objects and actions, personal pronouns, possessives, and descriptive terms) in interactions with adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Understanding Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Words
indicator.description: Use English words, mainly consisting of concrete nouns.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Using Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Words
indicator.description: Use varied English words, including an increasing number of concrete nouns and some verbs and pronouns.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Using Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Words
indicator.description: Use a wide variety of English words to share knowledge of concepts, including words across all parts of speech, with some inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Using Words

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Grammatical Features
indicator.description: Use one or two familiar English verbs as all-purpose verbs, with some inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Using Grammatical Features

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Grammatical Features
indicator.description: Use some grammatical rules in English, such as using -S or -es for plural nouns and -ing for verbs, sometimes with inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Using Grammatical Features

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Grammatical Features
indicator.description: Broaden the use of English grammatical rules, such as irregular plurals or simple past tense verbs, sometimes with inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Using Grammatical Features

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Recognize the first letter in their own name in the English alphabet.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Recognizing and Identifying Letters — Discovering — Recognize the first letter in their own name in the English alphabet.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Accurately identify a few letters of the alphabet in English.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Recognizing and Identifying Letters — Developing — Accurately identify a few letters of the alphabet in English.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Identifying Letters
indicator.description: Accurately identify many uppercase and some lowercase letters of the English alphabet.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Recognizing and Identifying Letters — Broadening — Accurately identify many uppercase and some lowercase letters of the English alphabet.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness that English alphabet letters have associated sounds.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence — Discovering — Demonstrate awareness that English alphabet letters have associated sounds.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence
indicator.description: Accurately identify or produce the sound associated with one or two letters in the English alphabet.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence — Developing — Accurately identify or produce the sound associated with one or two letters in the English alphabet.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence
indicator.description: Accurately identify or produce the sounds of a few letters of the English alphabet (about 5-10), with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Learning Letter-Sound Correspondence — Broadening — Accurately identify or produce the sounds of a few letters of the English alphabet (about 5-10), with adult support.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding the Concept of Print
indicator.description: Interact with materials representing the letters of the English alphabet.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Understanding the Concept of Print — Discovering — Interact with materials representing the letters of the English alphabet.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding the Concept of Print
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness of English print.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Understanding the Concept of Print — Developing — Demonstrate awareness of English print.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding the Concept of Print
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness that they are interacting with English print as something that can be read and has specific meaning.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Understanding the Concept of Print — Broadening — Demonstrate awareness that they are interacting with English print as something that can be read and has specific meaning.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Print Conventions
indicator.description: Engage and interact with English-language books.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Understanding Print Conventions — Discovering — Engage and interact with English-language books.

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Print Conventions
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding of a few general book-handling behaviors, such as turning pages from right to left and recognizing the front cover of a book.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Understanding Print Conventions — Developing — Demonstrate understanding of a few general book-handling behaviors...

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Print Conventions
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding of a few book-handling behaviors or print conventions specific to English, such as turning pages one at a time in a single direction. Demonstrate understanding that print in English is organized from left to right, top to bottom and that pages are turned from right to left when a book is read.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Understanding Print Conventions — Broadening

domain.name: English Language Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Words
indicator.description: Vocabulary. Discovering: Pay attention to English oral language and understand a few common English words, while relying mainly on intonation, facial expressions, and gestures of the speaker in interactions with adults and peers. Developing: Demonstrate understanding of words in English for objects and actions as well as phrases encountered frequently in interactions with adults and peers. Broadening: Demonstrate understanding of a larger set of words in English (for example, objects and actions, personal pronouns, possessives, and descriptive terms) in interactions with adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Understanding Words

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Words
indicator.description: Vocabulary. Discovering: Use English words, mainly consisting of concrete nouns. Developing: Use varied English words, including an increasing number of concrete nouns and some verbs and pronouns. Broadening: Use a wide variety of English words to share knowledge of concepts, including words across all parts of speech, with some inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Using Words

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Grammatical Features
indicator.description: Grammar. Discovering: Use one or two familiar English verbs as all-purpose verbs, with some inaccuracies. Developing: Use some grammatical rules in English, such as using -s or -es for plural nouns and -ing for verbs, sometimes with inaccuracies. Broadening: Broaden the use of English grammatical rules, such as irregular plurals or simple past tense verbs, sometimes with inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Using Grammatical Features

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Using Complex Sentence Structures
indicator.description: Grammar. Discovering: Use words or phrases repeatedly experienced in English to communicate. Developing: Use a few formulaic English sentence structures to communicate on a range of topics by switching out key words in a phrase. Broadening: Use a variety of English grammatical forms, with some inaccuracies, to produce many different types of sentence structures. Grammatical forms can include adding appropriate possessive pronouns (for example, your, my); conjunctions (for example, and, or); and other elements (for example, adjectives, adverbs).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Using Complex Sentence Structures

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Communicating Needs
indicator.description: Language Use. Discovering: Show increasing reliance on nonverbal communication with English speakers, such as gestures or behaviors, to seek attention, make a request, or initiate a response. Developing: Combine nonverbal communication and some English phrases to be understood by English speakers. Broadening: Use single English words and verbal communication in English to be understood by English speakers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Communicating Needs

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Understanding Requests and Directions
indicator.description: Language Use. Discovering: Follow simple directions in English when given additional contextual clues. Developing: Follow directions that involve repeatedly experienced routines or contextual clues. Broadening: Follow directions in English that involve more than one step, relying less on contextual clues.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Understanding Requests and Directions

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Asking Questions
indicator.description: Discovering: Use a frequently experienced question phrase (such as "What's that?") or use one or two English words with a rising pitch to ask questions. Developing: Use a few question structures as a formula, filling in different words to ask about various topics. Broadening: Use "who," "what," "why," "how," "when," and "where" to produce questions in many forms to ask about a variety of topics in English.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Asking Questions

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Constructing Narratives
indicator.description: Discovering: Identify parts of real or fictional narratives using a few English vocabulary words. Developing: Construct real or fictional short narratives using English vocabulary in a few simple English sentence structures. Broadening: Construct real or fictional narratives by stringing together sentences with varied structures in English.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Constructing Narratives

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Sharing Explanations and Opinions
indicator.description: Discovering: Use English vocabulary and gestures to share descriptions, opinions, and explanations. Developing: Use a few simple sentence structures to share descriptions, opinions, and explanations. Broadening: Use varied sentence structures to share descriptions, opinions, and explanations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Sharing Explanations and Opinions

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Participating in Conversations
indicator.description: Discovering: Engage in conversation with English speakers, by mostly listening and responding with a few English words, gestures, or other nonverbal responses. Developing: Converse with others using frequently heard vocabulary in English, often using short, commonly used sentences and phrases and a few repeated grammatical structures, sometimes with inaccuracies. Broadening: Engage in conversations in English using increasingly complex vocabulary and varied grammatical structures, sometimes with inaccuracies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Participating in Conversations

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Segmenting Sounds
indicator.description: Phonological Awareness. Discovering: Recognize and produce sounds of spoken English. Developing: Isolate and pronounce the first sound of a word in English, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects. Broadening: Match English words that have the same first sound in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Recognizing and Segmenting Sounds

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Recognizing and Blending Sounds
indicator.description: Phonological Awareness. Discovering: Recognize and produce sounds of spoken English. Developing: When presented with two syllables and individual sounds, blend them into English words in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects. Broadening: When presented with single-syllable English words (such as "sand" and "box"), blend them into a compound word in speech, with adult support or the support of pictures or objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Recognizing and Blending Sounds

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
domain.description: This is one out of two sub-domains in the Language and Literacy Development domain.

indicator.name: Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay
indicator.description: Phonological Awareness. Discovering: Repeat or recite parts of simple songs, poems, and fingerplays. Developing: Attend to and participate in frequently experienced songs, poems, and fingerplays. Broadening: Repeat, recite, produce, or initiate simple songs, poems, and fingerplays.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Participating in Rhyming and Wordplay

domain.name: Language and Literacy Development
