indicator.name: Writing Own Name
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Write marks to represent own name. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Write own name nearly correctly. Refer to the full draft of the PTKLF for additional information on how this foundation develops depending on the languages children are learning.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Writing Own Name

indicator.name: Reciting Numbers
indicator.description: Recite numbers in order from one to ten or higher with no more than a few errors.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Reciting Numbers — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Reciting Numbers
indicator.description: Recite numbers in order from one to thirty with no more than a few errors. Count forward from a number other than one.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Reciting Numbers — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: One-to-One Correspondence
indicator.description: Count five objects or more using one-to-one correspondence (one object for each number word).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 One-to-One Correspondence — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: One-to-One Correspondence
indicator.description: Count ten objects or more using one-to-one correspondence (one object for each number word).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 One-to-One Correspondence — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Cardinality
indicator.description: Answer the question "How many?" by counting. May repeat the last number word in the number list after counting but is still developing an understanding that the number name of the last object counted represents the total number of objects in the group.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Cardinality — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Cardinality
indicator.description: Consistently demonstrate understanding when counting that the number name of the last object counted represents the total number of objects in the group.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Cardinality — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Subitize
indicator.description: Identify without counting the number of objects in a small collection (for example, one to four objects).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Subitize — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Subitize
indicator.description: Identify without counting the number of objects in a collection of one to five objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Subitize — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Numeral Recognition
indicator.description: Recognize and name a few written numerals under 10.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Numeral Recognition — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Numeral Recognition
indicator.description: Recognize and name all written numerals through 10.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Numeral Recognition — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Number Comparison
indicator.description: Compare two groups of objects that are clearly equal or different in size and communicate, "same" or "more."

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Number Comparison — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Number Comparison
indicator.description: Compare (with or without counting) two groups of objects by counting and communicating, "more," "same," "less," or "fewer."

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Number Comparison — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Principles of Addition and Subtraction
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding that adding one or taking away one object changes the number in a small group of objects by exactly one.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Principles of Addition and Subtraction — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Principles of Addition and Subtraction
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding that adding or taking away one or more objects from a group will increase or decrease the number of objects in the group.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Principles of Addition and Subtraction — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Number Composition and Decomposition
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding that a set of objects is made up of smaller parts and that the whole set is bigger than its parts. Combine two small sets to create a larger set (for example, 3 and 2 to make a set of 5).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Number Composition and Decomposition — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Number Composition and Decomposition
indicator.description: Decompose a set of objects into two small sets in more than one way (for example, decompose 5 into sets of 3 and 2, or 1 and 4).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Number Composition and Decomposition — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Solving Addition and Subtraction Problems
indicator.description: Solve addition and subtraction problems with a very small number of objects in the context of everyday situations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Solving Addition and Subtraction Problems — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Solving Addition and Subtraction Problems
indicator.description: Solve addition and subtraction problems with a larger number of objects (sums up to 10) in the context of everyday situations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Solving Addition and Subtraction Problems — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Sharing Objects (Division)
indicator.description: Share a small number of objects (for example, four or six objects) equally between two recipients.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Sharing Objects (Division) ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Share a small number of objects (for example, four or six objects) equally between two recipients.

indicator.name: Sharing Objects (Division)
indicator.description: Share a slightly larger number of objects equally between two or more recipients (for example, nine objects among three recipients).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Sharing Objects (Division) ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Share a slightly larger number of objects equally between two or more recipients (for example, nine objects among three recipients).

indicator.name: Sorting and Classifying
indicator.description: Notice similarities and differences in the attributes of objects. Sort and classify objects by one attribute into two or more groups.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Sorting and Classifying ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Notice similarities and differences in the attributes of objects. Sort and classify objects by one attribute into two or more groups.

indicator.name: Sorting and Classifying
indicator.description: Sort and classify objects by one or more attributes into two or more groups with accuracy and flexibility. When sorting by two attributes, a child may first sort by one attribute and then by the second attribute.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Sorting and Classifying ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Sort and classify objects by one or more attributes into two or more groups with accuracy and flexibility.

indicator.name: Recognizing, Duplicating, and Extending Patterns
indicator.description: Notice and explore patterns in their environment and, with adult support, duplicate simple repeating patterns (for example, ABAB).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Recognizing, Duplicating, and Extending Patterns ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice and explore patterns in their environment and, with adult support, duplicate simple repeating patterns (for example, ABAB).

indicator.name: Recognizing, Duplicating, and Extending Patterns
indicator.description: Explore, extend, and duplicate a variety of repeating patterns (for example, AABBAABB, ABCABC) with adult support. Describe the repeating part of a pattern (pattern unit).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Recognizing, Duplicating, and Extending Patterns ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Explore, extend, and duplicate a variety of repeating patterns (for example, AABBAABB, ABCABC) with adult support. Describe the repeating part of a pattern (pattern unit).

indicator.name: Creating Patterns
indicator.description: Create, with adult support, a simple repeating pattern (for example, ABAB).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Creating Patterns ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Create, with adult support, a simple repeating pattern (for example, ABAB).

indicator.name: Creating Patterns
indicator.description: Create a variety of repeating patterns (for example, AABBAABB, ABCABC) or recreate existing patterns using different objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Creating Patterns ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Create a variety of repeating patterns (for example, AABBAABB, ABCABC) or recreate existing patterns using different objects.

indicator.name: Comparing Measurable Attributes of Objects
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness that objects can be compared by length, weight, or capacity by noticing differences in objects and communicating about their comparison.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Comparing Measurable Attributes of Objects ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate awareness that objects can be compared by length, weight, or capacity by noticing differences in objects and communicating about their comparison.

indicator.name: Comparing Measurable Attributes of Objects
indicator.description: Compare two objects by length, weight, or capacity (for example, putting objects side by side) and communicate about their comparison.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Comparing Measurable Attributes of Objects ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Compare two objects by length, weight, or capacity (for example, putting objects side by side) and communicate about their comparison.

indicator.name: Ordering Objects
indicator.description: Order a few objects (for example, three) by length or other attributes (for example, height, capacity).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Ordering Objects ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Order a few objects (for example, three) by length or other attributes (for example, height, capacity).

indicator.name: Ordering Objects
indicator.description: Order a slightly larger number of objects (for example, four or five) by length or other attributes (for example, height, capacity).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Ordering Objects ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Order a slightly larger number of objects (for example, four or five) by length or other attributes (for example, height, capacity).

indicator.name: Measuring Length
indicator.description: Measure length using concrete objects laid end to end, sometimes needing adult support. Note: Children may not yet understand that units need to be of equal length.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Measuring Length ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) (No foundation) ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Measure length using concrete objects laid end to end, sometimes needing adult support.

indicator.name: Representing Data
indicator.description: Use objects, tally marks, or pictographs to represent data in two groups, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Representing Data ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Use objects, tally marks, or pictographs to represent data in two groups, with adult support.

indicator.name: Representing Data
indicator.description: Use objects, tally marks, or pictographs to represent data in two or more groups. Demonstrate understanding that each object, tally mark, or picture represents one data point.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Representing Data ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Use objects, tally marks, or pictographs to represent data in two or more groups. Demonstrate understanding that each object, tally mark, or picture represents one data point.

indicator.name: Interpreting Data
indicator.description: Notice, with adult support, differences in the number of data points in two or more groups. Determine which group has more or less.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Interpreting Data ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice, with adult support, differences in the number of data points in two or more groups. ... Determine which group has more or less.

indicator.name: Interpreting Data
indicator.description: Describe and compare, with adult support, the data of two groups and describe which one has more or less.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Interpreting Data ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Describe and compare, with adult support, the data of two groups and describe which one has more or less.

indicator.name: Identifying Two-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Identify familiar two-dimensional shapes such as circle, square, triangle, and rectangle. Note: May not identify a nontypical version of a shape (for example, a square turned so that the point is down—a diamond).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Identifying Two-Dimensional Shapes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Identify familiar two-dimensional shapes such as circle, square, triangle, and rectangle.

indicator.name: Identifying Two-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Identify, describe, and construct different shapes including variations of circle, square, triangle, rectangle, and other shapes. Use informal language to describe defining properties of a shape (for example, sides, corners, round).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Identifying Two-Dimensional Shapes ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify, describe, and construct different shapes including variations of circle, square, triangle, rectangle, and other shapes.

indicator.name: Identifying Three-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Occasionally identify a few familiar three-dimensional shapes using informal names (for example, saying "ball" when referring to a sphere).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Identifying Three-Dimensional Shapes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Occasionally identify a few familiar three-dimensional shapes using informal names

indicator.name: Identifying Three-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Identify a few familiar three-dimensional shapes such as sphere, cube, and cylinder. Note: Sometimes still use informal names (for example, ball, square box, tube).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Identifying Three-Dimensional Shapes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Identify a few familiar three-dimensional shapes such as sphere, cube, and cylinder.

indicator.name: Comparing Two-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Compare two-dimensional shapes of different sizes and orientations to determine whether they are the same shape.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Comparing Two-Dimensional Shapes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Compare two-dimensional shapes of different sizes and orientations to determine whether they are the same shape.

indicator.name: Comparing Two-Dimensional Shapes
indicator.description: Compare two-dimensional shapes of different sizes and orientations to determine whether they are the same shape. Identify similarities and differences in the properties (number of sides or vertices) of two different shapes.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Comparing Two-Dimensional Shapes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Compare two-dimensional shapes... Identify similarities and differences in the properties

indicator.name: Composing Shapes
indicator.description: Use two- or three-dimensional shapes to represent different elements of a picture or design (for example, adding a circle in a corner to represent the sun).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Composing Shapes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Use two- or three-dimensional shapes to represent different elements of a picture or design

indicator.name: Composing Shapes
indicator.description: Combine different two- or three-dimensional shapes to create a picture or design (for example, make a house with two blocks shaped like rectangular prisms and one shaped like a triangular prism).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Composing Shapes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Combine different two- or three-dimensional shapes to create a picture or design

indicator.name: Positions and Directions in Space
indicator.description: Identify some positions of objects and people in space such as in/on, under/over, up/down, and inside/outside.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Positions and Directions in Space ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Identify some positions of objects and people in space such as in/on, under/over, up/down, and inside/outside.

indicator.name: Positions and Directions in Space
indicator.description: Identify positions of objects and people in space including in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside, near/far, next to, beside/between, and in front of/behind.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Positions and Directions in Space ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify positions of objects and people in space including in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside, near/far, next to, beside/between, and in front of/behind.

indicator.name: Mental Rotation
indicator.description: Rely on trial and error to determine how objects move in space and fit in different locations (for example, try to fit an object into a hole by rotating, flipping, or sliding the piece in different orientations until it fits).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Mental Rotation ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Rely on trial and error to determine how objects move in space and fit in different locations

indicator.name: Mental Rotation
indicator.description: Rotate, flip, or slide objects to solve a problem without relying as much on physical trial and error (for example, rotate an object before fitting it into a hole).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Mental Rotation ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Rotate, flip, or slide objects to solve a problem without relying as much on physical trial and error

indicator.name: Making Observations
indicator.description: Observe and actively explore objects and events using their senses and describe their observations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Observe and actively explore objects and events using their senses and describe their observations.

indicator.name: Making Observations
indicator.description: Observe and actively explore objects and events using their senses and describe their observations in greater detail.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Observe and actively explore objects and events using their senses and describe their observations in greater detail.

indicator.name: Comparing and Contrasting
indicator.description: Compare and contrast objects and events and describe similarities and differences based on observable properties.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Comparing and Contrasting ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Compare and contrast objects and events and describe similarities and differences based on observable properties.

indicator.name: Comparing and Contrasting
indicator.description: Compare and contrast objects and events based on physical properties and functions and describe similarities and differences in greater detail.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Comparing and Contrasting ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Compare and contrast objects and events based on physical properties and functions and describe similarities and differences in greater detail.

indicator.name: Asking Questions
indicator.description: Demonstrate curiosity and raise simple questions about objects and events in their environment.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Asking Questions ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate curiosity and raise simple questions about objects and events in their environment.

indicator.name: Asking Questions
indicator.description: Demonstrate curiosity and an increased ability to formulate specific and detailed questions about objects and events in their environment.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Asking Questions ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate curiosity and an increased ability to formulate specific and detailed questions about objects and events in their environment.

indicator.name: Defining Problems
indicator.description: Identify problems during play and everyday interactions and try simple solutions on their own or in collaboration with peers and adults.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Defining Problems ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Identify problems during play and everyday interactions and try simple solutions on their own or in collaboration with peers and adults.

indicator.name: Defining Problems
indicator.description: Identify problems during play and everyday interactions and try multistep solutions on their own or in collaboration with peers and adults.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Defining Problems ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify problems during play and everyday interactions and try multistep solutions on their own or in collaboration with peers and adults.

indicator.name: Making Predictions
indicator.description: Make simple predictions, give simple reasons for their predictions, and, with adult support, check the predictions through concrete experiences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Making Predictions ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Make simple predictions, give simple reasons for their predictions, and, with adult support, check the predictions through concrete experiences.

indicator.name: Making Predictions
indicator.description: Make more detailed predictions drawing on prior experiences and observations, create plans with adult support to check predictions, and demonstrate an emerging ability to discuss why predictions were correct or incorrect.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Making Predictions ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Make more detailed predictions drawing on prior experiences and observations, create plans with adult support to check predictions

indicator.name: Planning and Carrying Out Investigations
indicator.description: Carry out simple experiments or investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Planning and Carrying Out Investigations ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Carry out simple experiments or

indicator.name: Planning and Carrying Out Investigations
indicator.description: Carry out more complex experiments or investigations, on their own or in collaboration with peers and adults, with greater persistence. Use observations and results of prior explorations to generate new questions and test their hypotheses.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Planning and Carrying Out Investigations

indicator.name: Using Tools
indicator.description: Identify and use some observation and measurement tools, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Using Tools

indicator.name: Using Tools
indicator.description: Identify and more spontaneously use a greater variety of observation and measurement tools, with some adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Using Tools

indicator.name: Documenting Observations and Using Models
indicator.description: Record observations or findings with adult support and use simple representations, including drawings, models, movement, role-play, and other methods, to convey their observations and understanding of science concepts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Documenting Observations and Using Models

indicator.name: Documenting Observations and Using Models
indicator.description: Record observations or findings in greater detail with some adult support and use more elaborate representations, including drawings, models, charts, diagrams, movement, role-play, and other methods, to convey their observations and understanding of science concepts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Documenting Observations and Using Models

indicator.name: Mathematical Thinking and Analyzing Data
indicator.description: Use mathematical thinking to analyze and quantify their observations and answer questions that arise in everyday activities, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Mathematical Thinking and Analyzing Data

indicator.name: Mathematical Thinking and Analyzing Data
indicator.description: Use mathematical thinking with greater precision to analyze and quantify their observations and answer questions that arise in everyday activities, with some adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Mathematical Thinking and Analyzing Data

indicator.name: Formulating and Communicating Explanations and Solutions
indicator.description: Formulate and communicate simple explanations and solutions during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Formulating and Communicating Explanations and Solutions

indicator.name: Formulating and Communicating Explanations and Solutions
indicator.description: Formulate and communicate more detailed and precise explanations and solutions during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Formulating and Communicating Explanations and Solutions

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Characteristics of Objects and Materials
indicator.description: Investigate and describe the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid or nonsolid materials (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture, smell, and sound).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Characteristics of Objects and Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid or nonsolid materials

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Characteristics of Objects and Materials
indicator.description: Investigate and describe in greater detail the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid, liquid, or gas materials (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture, smell, and sound).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Characteristics of Objects and Materials ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe in greater detail the characteristics and physical properties of objects

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Light and Sound Waves
indicator.description: Notice and explore sound, light, and shadows using their senses and by manipulating objects and materials during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Light and Sound Waves ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Notice and explore sound, light, and shadows

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Light and Sound Waves
indicator.description: Explore and describe changes in the properties of sound, light, and shadows by manipulating different objects and materials during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Light and Sound Waves ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Explore and describe changes in the properties of sound, light, and shadows

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials
indicator.description: Explore and describe changes in objects and materials using their senses (for example, change in color, shape, texture, temperature) during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Explore and describe changes in objects and materials using their senses

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials
indicator.description: Explore, describe in greater detail, and explain changes in objects and materials using their senses (for example, change in color, shape, texture, form, temperature) during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Explore, describe in greater detail, and explain changes

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Force and Motion
indicator.description: Observe and describe the way objects' speed and direction change and explore the effect of their own actions (for example, pushing, pulling, rolling, dropping) on making objects move or stop during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Force and Motion ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Observe and describe the way objects' speed and direction change

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Force and Motion
indicator.description: Make and test predictions about how objects change direction, speed, or the distance they go and, based on their observations, explain why objects start, stop, or change direction or speed during play and collaborative investigations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Force and Motion ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Make and test predictions about how objects change direction

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Energy
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, that things (living and nonliving) need sources of energy to function.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Energy ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, that things (living and nonliving) need sources of energy

sub_strand.name: Changes in Nonliving Objects and Materials

indicator.name: Energy
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, of the different sources of energy that things (living and nonliving) need and describe the changes they observe as a result of these sources of energy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Energy ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, of the different sources of energy

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Characteristics of Living Things
indicator.description: Identify and describe characteristics of a variety of animals and plants, including appearance (inside and outside) and behavior, and demonstrate an emerging ability to categorize them.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Characteristics of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Identify and describe characteristics of a variety of animals and plants

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Characteristics of Living Things
indicator.description: Identify and describe characteristics of a greater variety of animals and plants and demonstrate an increased ability to categorize them.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Characteristics of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify and describe characteristics of a greater variety

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Bodily Processes
indicator.description: Indicate emerging knowledge of bodily processes (for example, eating, sleeping, breathing, walking) in humans and other animals.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Bodily Processes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Indicate emerging knowledge of bodily processes

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Bodily Processes
indicator.description: Indicate greater knowledge of bodily processes (for example, eating, sleeping, breathing, walking) in humans and other animals through more detailed observations and descriptions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Bodily Processes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Indicate greater knowledge of bodily processes

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Living and Nonliving Things
indicator.description: Expect animate objects (people and animals) to self-initiate movement and to have different insides and biological processes that make them behave differently from inanimate objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Living and Nonliving Things ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Expect animate objects (people and animals) to self-initiate movement

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Living and Nonliving Things
indicator.description: Indicate knowledge of the difference between living and nonliving things and recognize that only living things (people, animals, plants) undergo biological changes such as growth, illness, healing, and dying.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Living and Nonliving Things ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Indicate knowledge of the difference between living and nonliving things

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Heredity and Traits
indicator.description: Name and describe similarities and differences they observe between grown animals, including humans, and their young ones.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Heredity and Traits ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Name and describe similarities and differences

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Heredity and Traits
indicator.description: Express their expectation that young animals and plants will reflect similar characteristics to grown animals and plants of the same kind.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Heredity and Traits ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Express their expectation that young animals and plants will reflect similar characteristics

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Habitats
indicator.description: Identify the habitats of people and familiar animals and plants and communicate their understanding that living things have different habitats.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Habitats ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Identify the habitats of people and familiar animals and plants

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

indicator.name: Habitats
indicator.description: Recognize that living things have different habitats suited to their unique needs.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Habitats ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that living things have different habitats suited to their unique needs

indicator.name: Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things
indicator.description: Observe and explore growth in humans, animals, and plants and communicate an increased understanding that living things change as they grow and age.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Observe and explore growth in humans, animals, and plants

indicator.name: Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things
indicator.description: Observe and explore growth and changes in humans, animals, and plants and communicate an understanding that living things change over time in size and other transformations related to an individual's life cycle (for example, birth, growth, reproduction, death). Describe capacities as they grow and age.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Observe and explore growth and changes

indicator.name: Needs of Living Things
indicator.description: Recognize that animals and plants require care and show an emerging understanding that feeding and watering support the growth and survival of humans, animals, and plants.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Needs of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Recognize that animals and plants require care

indicator.name: Needs of Living Things
indicator.description: Describe the needs of humans, animals, and plants for growth and survival (for example, food, water, sleep, sunshine, shelter).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Needs of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Describe the needs of humans, animals, and plants

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Earth Materials and Objects

indicator.name: Characteristics of Earth Materials
indicator.description: Investigate and describe the characteristics (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture) of earth materials such as sand, rocks, soil, water, and air.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Characteristics of Earth Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics

sub_strand.name: Properties and Characteristics of Earth Materials and Objects

indicator.name: Characteristics of Earth Materials
indicator.description: Investigate and describe the characteristics of earth materials and compare and contrast materials based on their different features (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Characteristics of Earth Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics of earth materials and compare and contrast

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Natural Objects in the Sky
indicator.description: Observe and describe natural objects in the sky (sun, moon, stars, and clouds).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Observe and describe natural objects in the sky

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Natural Objects in the Sky
indicator.description: Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement and apparent changes in the sun, moon, stars, and clouds.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Weather
indicator.description: Notice and describe changes in weather. Notice the effects of weather and seasonal changes on their own lives and on plants and animals.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Notice and describe changes in weather

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Weather
indicator.description: Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples of the effects of changes in weather and seasons on their own lives and on plants and animals.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Earth and Human Activity
indicator.description: Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, and participate in activities related to caring for the environment.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment

sub_strand.name: Changes in the Earth and Space

indicator.name: Earth and Human Activity
indicator.description: Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, discuss in simple terms how to care for the environment, and participate in activities related to its care.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Engineering Design Process
indicator.description: Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating simple solutions to the problems they identify, and, with adult support, testing and refining their solutions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Engineering Design Process
indicator.description: Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating more detailed solutions to the problems they identify, and testing and refining their solutions with less adult support and over longer periods of time.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Design Solutions and Society
indicator.description: Notice and explore, with adult support, how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and goals in everyday life.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Design Solutions and Society Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Notice and explore, with adult support, how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and goals in everyday life.

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Design Solutions and Society
indicator.description: Explore in more detail how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and, with adult support, develop different solutions to address the needs of their families and communities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Design Solutions and Society Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Explore in more detail how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and, with adult support, develop different solutions to address the needs of their families and communities.

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Using Digital Devices
indicator.description: Demonstrate emerging understanding that different digital tools and devices serve different functions (for example, taking videos or photos) and use digital tools, with adult support, to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Using Digital Devices Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Demonstrate emerging understanding that different digital tools and devices serve different functions (for example, taking videos or photos) and use digital tools, with adult support, to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

strand.name: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

indicator.name: Using Digital Devices
indicator.description: Recognize a greater diversity of digital tools and devices and their function (for example, look up information, learn or practice a new skill) and use them with less adult support to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Using Digital Devices Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Recognize a greater diversity of digital tools and devices and their function (for example, look up information, learn or practice a new skill) and use them with less adult support to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

domain.name: Physical Development

indicator.name: Balancing While Still

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Balancing While Still Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show increased balance and control when holding still.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Balancing While Still
indicator.description: Maintain balance while holding still; sometimes may need assistance.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Balancing While Still Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Maintain balance while holding still; sometimes may need assistance.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.description: Show increased balance control while moving in different directions and when transitioning from one movement or position to another.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Balancing in Motion Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show increased balance control while moving in different directions and when transitioning from one movement or position to another.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Balancing in Motion Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Maintain balance while in motion when moving from one position to another or when changing directions, though balance may not be completely stable.

indicator.description: Walk with balance and swing their arms opposite their legs (for example, step with the right foot, swing their left arm forward). Exhibit narrower space between feet while walking.

domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Running Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Run with a short stride length and feet off the ground for a short period of time.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.description: Run with a longer stride length and each foot off the ground for a longer period of time. Show more control when stopping running. Swing their arm while stepping with the opposite leg more consistently.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Running Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Run with a longer stride length and each foot off the ground for a longer period of time.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Jumping Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Jump on two feet for height and distance, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Jumping
indicator.description: Jump on two feet for height and distance with increased competence, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface. Swing arms to propel themselves while jumping.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Jumping Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Jump on two feet for height and distance with increased competence

domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.description: Demonstrate a variety of new locomotor skills in a basic way that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, such as hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Varied Locomotor Skills Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Demonstrate a variety of new locomotor skills in a basic way
