domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Varied Locomotor Skills
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased ability in performing locomotor skills that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, including engagement in hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Varied Locomotor Skills Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Demonstrate increased ability in performing locomotor skills

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Gross Motor Manipulative Skills
indicator.description: Show gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet to interact with objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Gross Motor Manipulative Skills Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet to interact with objects.

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Gross Motor Manipulative Skills
indicator.description: Show increased ability to perform gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet with increased coordination to interact with objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Gross Motor Manipulative Skills Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Show increased ability to perform gross motor manipulative skills

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Fine Motor Manipulative Skills
indicator.description: Show some fine motor manipulation skills that involve using hands and fingers to interact with objects used in daily life.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Fine Motor Manipulative Skills Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Show some fine motor manipulation skills

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Fine Motor Manipulative Skills
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased fine motor manipulation skills using hands and fingers with increasing competence and precision to interact with objects needed for daily life.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Fine Motor Manipulative Skills Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Demonstrate increased fine motor manipulation skills

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Hand Preference
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Tend to show a preference for using one hand more than the other within a task and sometimes across multiple tasks. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate a consistent preference for using one hand more often than the other within and across different tasks.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Hand Preference

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Knowledge of Body Parts
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of the names of basic body parts. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of more and a greater variety of body parts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Knowledge of Body Parts

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Spatial Awareness
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use their own body as a reference point when locating or relating to other people or objects in their immediate environment. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use their own body, general space, and other people's space when locating or relating to other people or objects in their immediate environment.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Spatial Awareness

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Directional Understanding
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Distinguish the direction of movements that are up and down and to the side of the body. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Understand and distinguish between the sides of the body (without necessarily understanding right and left).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Directional Understanding

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Directional Movement
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Move forward and backward or up and down easily. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Change directions, moving forward and backward, side to side, or up and down, quickly and with more accuracy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Directional Movement

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Object Locations
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to place an object on top of or under something with some accuracy. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an ability to place an object or their own body in front of, to the side of, or behind something else with accuracy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Object Locations

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Physical Activity
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Initiate or engage in simple physical activities for a short to moderate period of time. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Initiate more complex physical activities for a sustained period of time.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Physical Activity

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

indicator.name: Cardiovascular Endurance
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage in frequent bursts of active play that involves increased activity of the heart, lungs, and vascular system. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Engage in sustained active play of increasing intensity that involves the heart, lungs, and vascular system.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Cardiovascular Endurance

domain.name: Physical Development
domain.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development

sub_strand.name: Muscular Strength, Muscular Endurance, and Flexibility

indicator.name: Strength, Endurance, and Flexibility
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage in active play activities that enhance muscular strength throughout the body, muscular endurance, and flexibility. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Engage in increased active play activities that enhance muscular strength throughout the body, muscular endurance, and flexibility.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Strength, Endurance, and Flexibility

indicator.name: Identifying and Naming Body Parts
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify and correctly name a few external body parts (for example, elbow, head, private body parts) and internal body parts (for example, bones, brain, heart) and demonstrate limited knowledge of their functions. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify and correctly name several external body parts (for example, elbow, head, private body parts) and a few internal body parts (for example, bones, brain, heart) and demonstrate more detailed knowledge of their functions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Identifying and Naming Body Parts

indicator.name: Communicating About Health Needs
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Communicate to an adult, with varying specificity and accuracy, about feeling uncomfortable, not feeling well, or a special health need. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Consistently communicate to an adult about feeling uncomfortable, not feeling well, or a special health need and can identify a solution.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Health Needs

indicator.name: Understanding the Role of Health Care Providers
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Communicate their basic understanding that health care providers keep people well and help them when they are not well. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Communicate more specific knowledge on how health care providers keep people well and help them when they are not well.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Understanding the Role of Health Care Providers

indicator.name: Recognizing and Communicating About Body Boundaries
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to recognize and communicate about body boundaries, including wanted touches (for example, hug from a peer, high five with a teacher) and unwanted touches (for example, hitting, pushing, inappropriate touches). Tend to follow trusted adults' guidance about body boundaries. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Recognize and communicate, with adult support, about body boundaries, including wanted touches (for example, hug from a peer, high five with a teacher) and unwanted touches (for example, hitting, pushing, inappropriate touches). More consistently and independently follow and use trusted adults' guidance about body boundaries.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Recognizing and Communicating About Body Boundaries

indicator.name: Identifying Foods
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify a few specific foods. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify a larger variety of foods and know some of the related food groups.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Identifying Foods

indicator.name: Communicating Fullness and Hunger
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an emerging ability to communicate their own fullness and hunger to an adult. Anticipate mealtime routines but show a limited capacity to wait for the next meal. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Communicate more consistently their fullness and hunger to an adult. Anticipate mealtime routines and wait a little longer for a meal.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Fullness and Hunger

indicator.name: Understanding a Variety of Foods
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an emerging understanding that eating a variety of food helps them grow and feel good. Choose familiar foods, at mealtimes, including familial and cultural foods. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an understanding that eating a variety of food helps the body grow and feel good. Choose from a greater variety of foods including familial and cultural foods, although occasionally are open to trying new foods.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Understanding a Variety of Foods

indicator.name: Recognizing the Body's Response to Physical Activity
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize, with adult support, the body's response to physical activity (for example, heart beating fast, sweating, needing water) and demonstrate an emerging understanding that being active is healthy. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Recognize, with less or limited adult support, the body's response to physical activity (for example, heart beating fast, sweating, needing water) and indicate the need to be physically active outdoors or indoors. More consistently and with less adult support indicate the need to be physically active outdoors or indoors.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Recognizing the Body's Response to Physical Activity

indicator.name: Recognizing and Indicating When Tired
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize, with adult support, when they are tired and express tiredness with a range of behaviors. Demonstrate limited ability to follow sleep routines consistently. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Indicate when they are tired with a range of behaviors and demonstrate an emerging ability to understand that sleep and rest are part of keeping their bodies healthy. Follow sleep and rest routines more consistently.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Recognizing and Indicating When Tired

indicator.name: Handwashing
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of some steps in the handwashing routine with adult instruction and modeling. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of most or all steps in the handwashing routine with limited adult instruction and modeling.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Handwashing

indicator.name: Preventing Infectious Diseases
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice) with adult instruction and modeling. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice) with limited adult instruction and modeling.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Preventing Infectious Diseases

indicator.name: Toothbrushing
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge and follow some steps of the toothbrushing routine with adult supervision and instruction. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge and follow more steps of the toothbrushing routine and demonstrate knowledge of when toothbrushing should be done with limited adult supervision and instruction.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Toothbrushing

indicator.name: Practicing Sun Safety
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Practice some sun-safe actions (for example, wearing sunscreen, drinking water) with adult support and guidance. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Practice sun-safe actions (for example, wearing sunscreen, drinking water) with less adult support and guidance.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Practicing Sun Safety

indicator.name: Following Safety Rules
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Follow indoor and outdoor safety rules (that is, any rules that protect children from danger, risk, or injury) with adult support and prompting. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Follow indoor and outdoor safety rules (that is, any rules that protect children from danger, risk, or injury) with less adult support and guidance.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Following Safety Rules

indicator.name: Following Emergency Routines
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to follow emergency routines (for example, fire drill, earthquake drill) after instruction and practice with adult support and guidance. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate increased independent ability to follow emergency routines (for example, fire drill) after instruction and practice with some adult guidance.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Following Emergency Routines

indicator.name: Following Transportation and Pedestrian Safety Rules
indicator.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show an emerging ability to follow and understand transportation and pedestrian safety rules with adult support and supervision (for example, look both ways before crossing the street, help buckle the harness straps in a car seat). Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show increased ability to follow transportation and pedestrian safety rules with adult instruction and supervision (for example, look both ways before crossing the street, help buckle the harness straps in a car seat).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Following Transportation and Pedestrian Safety Rules

indicator.name: Making Observations and Asking Questions
indicator.description: Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions). Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions

indicator.name: Making Observations and Asking Questions
indicator.description: Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicator.name: Making Observations and Asking Questions
indicator.description: Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicator.name: Gathering and Using Evidence
indicator.description: Gather information with adult support from resources (such as books and other media) related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions). Gather information during extended inquiries, with adult support, from a greater variety of resources (such as informational books, magazines, media, and community members) to generate answers related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Gathering and Using Evidence

indicator.name: Gathering and Using Evidence
indicator.description: Gather information with adult support from resources (such as books and other media) related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Gathering and Using Evidence — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Gather information with adult support from resources (such as books and other media) related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicator.name: Gathering and Using Evidence
indicator.description: Gather information during extended inquiries, with adult support, from a greater variety of resources (such as informational books, magazines, media, and community members) to generate answers related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Gathering and Using Evidence — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Gather information during extended inquiries...

sub_strand.name: Communicating Ideas About the Social World

indicator.name: Creating Representations
indicator.description: Create representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to show developing understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support. Create more detailed representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to deepen and share their understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Creating Representations

sub_strand.name: Communicating Ideas About the Social World

indicator.name: Creating Representations
indicator.description: Create representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to show developing understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Creating Representations — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Create representations...

sub_strand.name: Communicating Ideas About the Social World

indicator.name: Creating Representations
indicator.description: Create more detailed representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to deepen and share their understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Creating Representations — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Create more detailed representations...

indicator.name: Self-Identity
indicator.description: Show awareness of characteristics of self. Show greater awareness and understanding.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity

indicator.name: Self-Identity
indicator.description: Show awareness of characteristics of self, including ethnic, racial, linguistic, religious, gender, and ability identities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show awareness of characteristics of self...

indicator.name: Self-Identity
indicator.description: Show greater awareness and understanding of characteristics of self, including ethnic, racial, linguistic, religious, gender, and ability identities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show greater awareness and understanding of characteristics of self...

indicator.name: Membership in Communities
indicator.description: Exhibit awareness of the communities (for example, family, peer group) with whom they interact frequently.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Membership in Communities — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of the communities...

indicator.name: Membership in Communities
indicator.description: Exhibit awareness of their membership in varied communities, including communities with whom they interact occasionally (for example, sports team, extended family, faith community).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Membership in Communities — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of their membership in varied communities...

indicator.name: Awareness of Social Roles
indicator.description: Exhibit awareness of familiar everyday social roles (for example, parent, sibling, teacher, doctor).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Awareness of Social Roles — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of familiar everyday social roles...

indicator.name: Awareness of Social Roles
indicator.description: Exhibit awareness of broader social roles beyond the everyday social roles they typically encounter.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Awareness of Social Roles — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Exhibit awareness of broader social roles...

indicator.name: Exploring Cultural Communities
indicator.description: Explore the cultural groups (for example, racial, ethnic, religious, linguistic, ability) in which they participate and display curiosity about other people's practices.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Cultural Communities — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Explore the cultural groups...

indicator.name: Exploring Cultural Communities
indicator.description: Explore characteristics, practices, and traditions of cultural groups (for example, racial, ethnic, religious, linguistic, ability) beyond their own cultural communities, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Cultural Communities — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Explore characteristics, practices, and traditions...

indicator.name: Exploring Similarities and Differences
indicator.description: Notice similarities and differences in people and families they know (for example, race, ethnicity, language, gender expression, family structures, ability).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Similarities and Differences — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice similarities and differences...

indicator.name: Exploring Similarities and Differences
indicator.description: Identify and name similarities and differences among people and families they know (for example, race, ethnicity, language, gender expression, family structures, ability), as well as people they are exposed to through learning materials, media, and daily interactions, with adult prompting.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Similarities and Differences — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify and name similarities and differences...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups
indicator.description: Recognize members of their peer groups (for example, members of their classroom, team, or activity group) and include them in play and learning activities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize members of their peer groups...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups
indicator.description: Identify members of their peer groups (for example, members of their classroom, team, or activity group), and include them in collaborative play and learning activities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify members of their peer groups...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Showing Care and Offering Help
indicator.description: Notice the needs of other people (for example, adults, peers) and the physical space; show care by offering help and contributing.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Showing Care and Offering Help — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice the needs of other people...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Showing Care and Offering Help
indicator.description: Increasingly notice individual and group needs. Show care for others (for example, adults, peers) and for the physical space through actions like offering help, contributing, advocating, and leading.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Showing Care and Offering Help — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Increasingly notice individual and group needs...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Understanding Different Needs and Fairness
indicator.description: Notice and communicate that peers have different needs by offering different resources and taking varied actions. (Even so, they may become frustrated by differences in the distribution of materials or opportunities.)

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and communicate that peers have different needs...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

sub_strand.name: Fairness and Respect for Other People

indicator.name: Understanding Different Needs and Fairness
indicator.description: Demonstrate understanding that peers have different needs by working toward supporting rules and practices that allow for each other's needs to be met. (Even so, they may still become frustrated by differences in the distribution of materials or opportunities.)

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate understanding that peers have different needs...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Contributing to the Group
indicator.description: Contribute to group efforts alongside adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Contribute to group efforts alongside adults and peers.

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Contributing to the Group
indicator.description: Contribute ideas, work toward group efforts, and show awareness of their individual contribution to collective group projects alongside adults and peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Contribute ideas, work toward group efforts...

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Following Community Rules and Norms
indicator.description: Recognize community rules and norms and, with adult support, follow rules while learning to coordinate personal interests with those of others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize community rules and norms and, with adult support, follow rules while learning

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Following Community Rules and Norms
indicator.description: Recognize rules and norms and follow community rules with fewer adult reminders. Enforce community rules and norms with peers.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize rules and norms and follow community rules with fewer adult reminders.

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Group Decision-Making
indicator.description: Participate in group decision-making (for example, voting, group meetings, circle time) with adult support. Express ideas of agreement and disagreement during decision-making practices.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Group Decision-Making

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Group Decision-Making
indicator.description: Participate in group decision-making (for example, voting, group meetings, circle time) about everyday choices that affect the group, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Group Decision-Making

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Collective Problem-Solving
indicator.description: Participate in problem-solving with peers (for example, interpersonal conflict resolution). Use simple bargaining or seek out adult intervention as strategies.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Collective Problem-Solving

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Collective Problem-Solving
indicator.description: Solve problems with peers through strategies like negotiation and compromise to solve problems. Intervene on behalf of one another during problem-solving.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Collective Problem-Solving

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Developing Solutions and Taking Action
indicator.description: Offer simple ideas to address problems and issues affecting their group, school, or larger community, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Developing Solutions and Taking Action

strand.name: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

indicator.name: Developing Solutions and Taking Action
indicator.description: Develop solutions and take action, often engaging others, to address problems and issues in their group, school, or larger community, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Developing Solutions and Taking Action

indicator.name: Using Time Order Words
indicator.description: Use sequential words or phrases, such as "before" and "after," to describe the time order of everyday personal events.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Using Time Order Words

indicator.name: Using Time Order Words
indicator.description: Indicate time order with increasing accuracy and sophistication. Use words like "before," "after," "then," "next," "yesterday," and "tomorrow."

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Using Time Order Words

indicator.name: Describing Change Over Time
indicator.description: Identify change over time in themself, with some adult support, when recalling events from early life (for example, "When I was a baby.").

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Describing Change Over Time

indicator.name: Describing Change Over Time
indicator.description: Identify and describe change over time in themself, family, and community and share more detailed stories about recent experiences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Describing Change Over Time

sub_strand.name: Historical Changes in People and the World

indicator.name: Recalling Past Events
indicator.description: Recall past experiences easily and enjoy hearing stories about the past but require adult help to distinguish when past events occurred in relation to each other and to connect them with current experience.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events

sub_strand.name: Historical Changes in People and the World

indicator.name: Recalling Past Events
indicator.description: Show improved ability to relate past events to other past events and current experiences but occasionally rely on some adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events

indicator.name: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
indicator.description: Identify the characteristics of familiar locations, such as home and school and describe objects and activities associated with each.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations

indicator.name: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
indicator.description: Identify characteristics of their community and region, including geographic features, weather, and common activities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations

indicator.name: Communicating Locations and Directions
indicator.description: Communicate location and directionality (for example, in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside) when describing nearby places and locations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions

indicator.name: Communicating Locations and Directions
indicator.description: Communicate a greater diversity of directions to others (for example, near/far, next to, beside/between, in front/behind), including specific places and locations that are farther away.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions

indicator.name: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
indicator.description: Demonstrate an emerging understanding that drawings, globes, building materials, and maps can represent the physical world, but often interpret map symbols imprecisely.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps

indicator.name: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
indicator.description: Create their own drawings, maps, and models; use globes, maps, and map symbols and use maps for basic problem-solving (for example, locating objects) more skillfully, with adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps

sub_strand.name: Caring for the Natural and Built World

indicator.name: Caring for the World
indicator.description: Show an interest in the natural and built world, including animals, plants, and the built environment, especially as they experience it directly. With adult support, develop understanding of the interaction between humans and the environment, including taking care of the natural world (for example, not polluting a lake).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.4 Caring for the World ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Show an interest in the natural and built world, including animals, plants, and the built environment...

sub_strand.name: Caring for the Natural and Built World

indicator.name: Caring for the World
indicator.description: Show an interest in the natural and built world both within and outside direct experience. Understand the positive and negative impacts of human interaction with the natural world. With adult support, develop actions to take care of the natural world and humans impacted by natural disasters (for example, forest fires).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 5.4 Caring for the World ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Show an interest in the natural and built world both within and outside direct experience...

indicator.name: Meeting Community Needs
indicator.description: Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and that there are different ways to meet those needs.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.1 Meeting Community Needs ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and that there are different ways to meet those needs.

indicator.name: Meeting Community Needs
indicator.description: Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and demonstrates emerging ability to identify people and places within the community that help community members meet their needs.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.1 Meeting Community Needs ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and demonstrates emerging ability to identify people and places within the community that help community members meet their needs.

indicator.name: Awareness of People at Work
indicator.description: Understand that people do different types of work, both inside and outside the home.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.2 Awareness of People at Work ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Understand that people do different types of work, both inside and outside the home.

indicator.name: Awareness of People at Work
indicator.description: Explore with adult support a wider array of work that people do both inside and outside the home. Understand how different types of work help communities meet their needs.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.2 Awareness of People at Work ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Explore with adult support a wider array of work that people do both inside and outside the home. Understand how different types of work help communities meet their needs.

indicator.name: Understanding Exchange
indicator.description: Recognize use of different forms of exchange, including trading and using money to buy and sell.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.3 Understanding Exchange ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Recognize use of different forms of exchange, including trading and using money to buy and sell.

indicator.name: Understanding Exchange
indicator.description: Understand more complex exchange concepts, like choosing one item over another (opportunity cost), limited access to or limited amounts of a resource (supply and demand), and the exchange of money to buy and sell goods and services.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 6.3 Understanding Exchange ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Understand more complex exchange concepts...

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts
indicator.description: Attend to and engage with different visual art styles and forms (such as paintings, sculptures, and collages), some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Attend to and engage with different visual art styles and forms...

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a variety of visual art styles and forms (such as paintings, sculptures, and collages), some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a variety of visual art styles and forms...

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art

indicator.name: Communicating About Art Forms and Elements
indicator.description: Notice and communicate about some objects, forms, or representations that appear in art.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Art Forms and Elements ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice and communicate about some objects, forms, or representations that appear in art.

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art

indicator.name: Communicating About Art Forms and Elements
indicator.description: Notice and communicate about specific elements that appear in art (such as color, line, texture, or perspective), and describe how objects, forms, or representations are positioned in the artwork.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Art Forms and Elements ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Notice and communicate about specific elements that appear in art...

indicator.name: Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves
indicator.description: Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes to create drawings or paintings that suggest people, animals, or other objects.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes to create drawings or paintings that suggest people, animals, or other objects.

indicator.name: Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves
indicator.description: Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes with increased precision and detail to create drawings or paintings of people, animals, or other objects that are mostly recognizable.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes with increased precision and detail...

indicator.name: Working with Dough or Clay
indicator.description: Make somewhat regular-shaped balls and coils out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Working with Dough or Clay ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Make somewhat regular-shaped balls and coils out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

indicator.name: Working with Dough or Clay
indicator.description: Make representational forms that are mostly recognizable out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Working with Dough or Clay ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Make representational forms that are mostly recognizable out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

indicator.name: Using Visual Arts Materials
indicator.description: Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art...

indicator.name: Using Visual Arts Materials
indicator.description: Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures) that is more detailed.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art...

indicator.name: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

indicator.name: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

indicator.name: Demonstrating Motor Control
indicator.description: Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicator.name: Demonstrating Motor Control
indicator.description: Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicator.name: Mixing and Blending Colors
indicator.description: Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

indicator.name: Mixing and Blending Colors
indicator.description: Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
indicator.description: Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
indicator.description: Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Intensity and Mood
indicator.description: Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Intensity and Mood
indicator.description: Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.

indicator.name: Using Visual Arts Materials
indicator.description: Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).

indicator.name: Using Visual Arts Materials
indicator.description: Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures) that is more detailed.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art...

indicator.name: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

indicator.name: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

indicator.name: Demonstrating Motor Control
indicator.description: Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicator.name: Demonstrating Motor Control
indicator.description: Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

indicator.name: Mixing and Blending Colors
indicator.description: Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

indicator.name: Mixing and Blending Colors
indicator.description: Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
indicator.description: Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
indicator.description: Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Intensity and Mood
indicator.description: Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

indicator.name: Intensity and Mood
indicator.description: Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Music
indicator.description: Attend to and engage with different sounds, vibrations, rhythms, and instruments, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Attending to and Engaging in Music - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Music
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a wider variety of sounds, vibrations, rhythms, and instruments, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Attending to and Engaging in Music - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music

indicator.name: Responding to Music with Body Movements
indicator.description: Use body movement to respond to music by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Responding to Music with Body Movements - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music

indicator.name: Responding to Music with Body Movements
indicator.description: Use body movement that more closely responds to the beat, dynamics (louds and softs), and tempo (speed) of music by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Responding to Music with Body Movements - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations
indicator.description: Notice and communicate differences between some vocal, instrumental, and environmental sounds and vibrations.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations
indicator.description: Notice and communicate differences between a larger array of vocal, instrumental, and environmental sounds and vibrations with increased accuracy.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments
indicator.description: Explore vocally and with instruments; sing patterns, chants, and parts of songs by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments
indicator.description: Expand vocal and instrumental exploration; sing patterns, chants, and entire songs alone or with others in varying pitches (highs and lows), dynamics (louds and softs), moods, and intensities.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness
indicator.description: Tap beats and rhythms with their hands, feet, and instruments or other percussive tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness
indicator.description: Increase complexity and accuracy in tapping various beats and rhythms with their hands, feet, and instruments or other percussive tools.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Communicating Music Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name basic terms and elements related to music, such as the names of a few instruments or a few basic terms describing tempo (speed) or dynamics (louds and softs).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Communicating Music Terms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Communicating Music Terms
indicator.description: Recognize and name a wider range of instruments and specific qualities of music, such as pitch (high or low), mood, tempo (speed), and dynamics (louds and softs).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Communicating Music Terms - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Music

indicator.name: Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms
indicator.description: Use vocal skills, instruments, and other tools to produce short melodies, chants, or songs using simple rhythms and tones, by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Music

indicator.name: Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms
indicator.description: Use vocal skills, instruments, and other tools to produce or improvise on melodies, chants, or songs using more complex rhythms and tones, by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama

indicator.name: Engaging in Drama
indicator.description: Engage in pretend play by themself or with others, acting out scripts, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Engaging in Drama - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama

indicator.name: Engaging in Drama
indicator.description: Engage in extended and flexible pretend play scenarios, by themself or with others, acting out scripts, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Engaging in Drama - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama

indicator.name: Understanding Plot
indicator.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the simple plot of a participatory drama.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Plot - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama

indicator.name: Understanding Plot
indicator.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the extended plot and conflict of a participatory drama.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Plot - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Showing Emotions
indicator.description: Use gestures, expressions, and movements to intentionally show different emotions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Showing Emotions - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Showing Emotions
indicator.description: Use gestures, expressions, and movements to intentionally show a wider range of emotions.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Showing Emotions - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

indicator.name: Acting Out Prompts or Scripts
indicator.description: Participate in acting out prompts or simple scripts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Acting Out Prompts or Scripts - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Acting Out Prompts or Scripts
indicator.description: Participate in acting out longer or more complex prompts or scripts.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Acting Out Prompts or Scripts - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

indicator.name: Engaging in Role-Play
indicator.description: Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a limited range of voices, movements, and gestures.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Engaging in Role-Play ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a limited range of voices, movements, and gestures.

indicator.name: Engaging in Role-Play
indicator.description: Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a wider range of voices, movements, and gestures.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Engaging in Role-Play ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a wider range of voices, movements, and gestures.

indicator.name: Vocal Projection
indicator.description: Project their voice with some clarity using some dynamics (louds and softs).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Vocal Projection ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Project their voice with some clarity using some dynamics (louds and softs).

indicator.name: Vocal Projection
indicator.description: Project their voice with increased clarity using a range of dynamics (louds and softs).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Vocal Projection ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Project their voice with increased clarity using a range of dynamics (louds and softs).

indicator.name: Communicating Drama Terms
indicator.description: Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to drama.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Communicating Drama Terms ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to drama.

indicator.name: Communicating Drama Terms
indicator.description: Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to drama and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Communicating Drama Terms ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to drama and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

indicator.name: Using Props or Costumes
indicator.description: Add props or costumes to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Using Props or Costumes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Add props or costumes to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

indicator.name: Using Props or Costumes
indicator.description: Intentionally use a variety of props, costumes, or scenery to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Using Props or Costumes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally use a variety of props, costumes, or scenery to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

indicator.name: Creating Scripts
indicator.description: Create new scripts, reinvent, or embellish existing stories and act them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.9 Creating Scripts ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Create new scripts, reinvent, or embellish existing stories and act them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

indicator.name: Creating Scripts
indicator.description: Intentionally create longer and more detailed scripts and stories, acting them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 3.9 Creating Scripts ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally create longer and more detailed scripts and stories, acting them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Dance

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Dance
indicator.description: Attend to or engage in different movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Attending to and Engaging in Dance ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Attend to or engage in different movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

sub_strand.name: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Dance

indicator.name: Attending to and Engaging in Dance
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased attention to or engagement with a variety of movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Attending to and Engaging in Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate increased attention to or engagement with a variety of movements, gestures, and expressions...

indicator.name: Spatial Awareness and Coordination
indicator.description: Demonstrate awareness of self and others in dance or when moving in space.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Spatial Awareness and Coordination ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Demonstrate awareness of self and others in dance or when moving in space.

indicator.name: Spatial Awareness and Coordination
indicator.description: Demonstrate increased awareness of self and others and coordination of movement in dance or when moving in space.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Spatial Awareness and Coordination Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate increased awareness of self and others and coordination of movement in dance or when moving in space.

indicator.name: Responding to Tempo
indicator.description: Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Responding to Tempo ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement.

indicator.name: Responding to Tempo
indicator.description: Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement with increased accuracy and skill.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Responding to Tempo Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement with increased accuracy and skill.

indicator.name: Learning Basic Dance Skills
indicator.description: Respond to the instruction of one skill at a time in movement (such as jumping or falling).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Learning Basic Dance Skills ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Respond to the instruction of one skill at a time in movement (such as jumping or falling).

indicator.name: Learning Basic Dance Skills
indicator.description: Respond to the instruction of more than one skill at a time in movement (such as turning, leaping, and turning again).

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Learning Basic Dance Skills Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Respond to the instruction of more than one skill at a time in movement (such as turning, leaping, and turning again).

indicator.name: Communicating Dance Terms
indicator.description: Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to dance.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Communicating Dance Terms ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to dance.

indicator.name: Communicating Dance Terms
indicator.description: Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to dance (such as steps, tempo [speed], or types of dances) and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Communicating Dance Terms Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to dance (such as steps, tempo [speed], or types of dances) and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Representation Through Dance
indicator.description: Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Representation Through Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Representation Through Dance
indicator.description: Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize with increased skill.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Representation Through Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize with increased skill.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Inventing and Improvising Dance
indicator.description: Invent dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.7 Inventing and Improvising Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Invent dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Inventing and Improvising Dance
indicator.description: Invent and recreate dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others. Often initiate a sequence of movements or steps.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.7 Inventing and Improvising Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Invent and recreate dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others. Often initiate a sequence of movements or steps.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Communicating Feelings Through Dance
indicator.description: Communicate simple feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement, by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.8 Communicating Feelings Through Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Communicate simple feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement, by themself or with others.

sub_strand.name: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

indicator.name: Communicating Feelings Through Dance
indicator.description: Communicate more complex feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement by themself or with others.

indicators.source_text: Foundation 4.8 Communicating Feelings Through Dance Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Communicate more complex feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement by themself or with others.
